title: put() without ?key= silently uses 'value' as key name (undocumented)
description: lib.rs:164: key_name defaults to 'value' when self.key is None. This is not documented in the README, doc comment, or inline comments. A user calling put() without ?key= then inspecting the Secret via kubectl will see data.value and not understand why. A user who previously stored a key named 'db-pass' and calls put()-no-key will overwrite their Secret structure. The default key name should be documented prominently. — Risk: repeating class of user issue reports.
